title: LLM Output Content Policy Violation Logging

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: Optional accelerated CIM Alerts correlation via tstats join on application identifiers.

dataSources: index=ai_ops sourcetype=azure:openai content filter annotations; index=ai_ops sourcetype=openai:api explicit /v1/moderations responses; index=security sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail eventName=ApplyGuardrail for Amazon Bedrock Guardrails; index=ai_ops sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety and sourcetype=gcp:vertex:audit for Google Vertex AI safety signals; index=ai_ops sourcetype=anthropic:api policy or moderation structured lines; index=ai_hec sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation internal proxy OTLP or JSON moderation hooks; KV lookup llm_app_inventory.csv with app_id, owner, tenant, classification, is_external_facing, red_team_window; accelerated Splunk CIM Alerts for sibling enterprise detections keyed on Alerts.dest harmonized with app_id.
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure, Splunk Add-on for AWS, custom Pub/Sub or HEC collectors for Vertex AI logs, optional OpenTelemetry to HEC for gateway moderation spans, Splunk Connect for Splunk HTTP Event Collector
description: Provider-side hate, sexual, violence, self-harm, jailbreak, prompt-injection, PII, and custom enterprise taxonomy hits must meet organization policy, brand safety, and abuse programs—not only model performance. This use case fuses Azure OpenAI content filter outcomes, OpenAI Moderation API classifications, Amazon Bedrock Guardrails interventions, Vertex Gemini safety categories, Anthropic policy signals where logged, and internal gateway moderation decisions so teams separate malicious misuse from benign regulated text, document audit evidence aligned to EU AI Act, NIS2, and NYDFS expectations, and triage red-team exercises differently from customer-impacting production blocks. It explicitly tracks prompt-side versus completion-side enforcement, blocked versus flagged dispositions, severity ladders returned by vendors, child outcomes from a centralized policy engine, and metadata-only storage so DLP and privacy reviews stay satisfied without retaining raw blocked completions.
value: Compliance and audit teams gain chronological, joinable evidence that safety controls activated with identifiable request and model context, which supports regulator questions about proportionate mitigation and traceability. Product and platform owners can tune system prompts, retrieval corpora, and locale-specific lexicons when MEDIUM severities cluster instead of blaming model quality alone. Security operations correlate elevated filter rates with Splunk CIM Alerts on the same application identifiers to distinguish coordinated abuse from provider threshold drift after silent vendor updates. Trust and safety analysts benchmark weekly category mixes against thirty-day baselines, escalate external-facing regressions early, and prove that only metadata—not sensitive generations—entered the log tier, shrinking insider risk from over-collection.
grandmaExplanation: Picture the automatic referee flags on AI answers: we record which rule tripped, how serious it was, and which customer-facing app was involved—without saving the embarrassing sentence itself. That way safety, legal, and product teams can fix policies calmly and show auditors the controls worked.
implementation: Ingest azure:openai, openai:api moderation calls, aws:cloudtrail ApplyGuardrail, vertex:gemini-safety or gcp:vertex:audit, anthropic:api policy lines, and llm:gateway:moderation HEC into ai_ops, security, or ai_hec; normalize fields; maintain llm_app_inventory.csv; accelerate Alerts summaries; schedule security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation every fifteen minutes; page CRITICAL and HIGH to the AI risk officer; archive weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index without raw text.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with AI platform engineering, trust and safety partners, data governance counsel, and the teams that operate Azure OpenAI Service diagnostic exports, Amazon Bedrock guardrails, Google Vertex AI safety telemetry, OpenAI organization projects, Anthropic workspace policies, and internal LLM reverse proxies. This use case is intentionally narrower than UC-13.4.1 latency service levels, UC-13.4.2 token cost economics, UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty cycles, UC-13.4.4 model artifact versioning, UC-13.4.5 gateway rate fairness, UC-13.4.6 self-hosted Ollama abuse, UC-13.4.7 MCP broker misuse, UC-13.4.10 API key rotation evidence, UC-13.4.13 sequence-to-sequence DSDL lifecycle attestations, and UC-13.4.15 MLTK statistical drift narratives. The objective is tamper-evident logging of provider-returned content filter and moderation outcomes plus enterprise gateway decisions: normalized policy categories such as hate, sexual, violence, self-harm, jailbreak, prompt injection, PII, and custom enterprise taxonomy nodes; severity ladders; blocked versus flagged-only dispositions; prompt-side versus completion-side evaluation; child records emitted by a central policy engine; and correlation identifiers that replay cleanly in audits for EU AI Act readiness, NIS2 operational evidence, NYDFS-style third-line reviews, and OECD AI Principle traceability without persisting raw blocked generations.

Scope boundaries: Splunk stores metadata suitable for DLP programs—category codes, numeric scores when vendors expose them, request identifiers, model identifiers, tenant or subscription identifiers, application identifiers, and redacted length or cryptographic hashes of prompts only where legal approves—not full prompt or completion text that tripped a filter. Legal and privacy teams must sign off on any hash strategy so reversible fingerprints do not appear. Provider account scoping requires separate Splunk roles per regulated line of business so a retail chatbot incident never mixes with healthcare copilot telemetry. Azure Diagnostic Settings should land content filtering annotations in Event Hub or storage accounts consumed by Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure paths per your reference architecture. Amazon Bedrock ApplyGuardrail outcomes belong in organization CloudTrail with S3 lifecycle aligned to evidence retention. Vertex AI safety signals may arrive through Pub/Sub sinks, Cloud Logging exports, or custom OpenTelemetry spans forwarded with HEC. OpenAI Moderation API calls should be logged at the gateway with explicit path markers so they are not confused with chat completions. Internal gateways should emit OTLP or JSON lines with a stable schema version field.

Configuration depth: in Azure OpenAI, document configured severity thresholds per harm category and whether asynchronous content filtering is enabled. In Bedrock, version guardrail identifiers, topic policies, word filters, sensitive information filters, and contextual grounding rules. In Vertex, capture safety_settings blocks and blocked versus block_few_shot distinctions. For OpenAI, record which moderation model revision is pinned. Gateway hooks must run before billing meters when possible so blocked traffic still appears in governance indexes even if usage accounting drops.

RBAC: analysts searching these indexes require justification workflows; break-glass searches need additional approval. Retention tiers should separate hot investigation windows from cold compliance archives. Secrets for HEC tokens and cloud ingest principals rotate under UC-13.4.10 cadence. Time synchronization across clouds must stay within seconds so multisearch arms align on request_id correlation.

Risk briefing: a spike in flagged-but-not-blocked events may indicate provider threshold drift rather than malicious users; pair with model version and deployment identifiers. A sudden drop to zero moderation events from a historically noisy external-facing app may mean logging broke, not that abuse stopped—treat silence as a monitoring failure mode.

Differentiation recap: content policy outcome telemetry with enterprise inventory enrichment—not cryptographic key rotation, not pure latency, not FinOps token charts, not local Ollama misuse patterns, not MCP tool exfiltration alone, not deep learning autoencoder health, not generic MLTK residual drift.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A, Azure OpenAI content filtering: enable diagnostic settings on the Azure OpenAI resource to stream audit and request metadata that includes content filter annotations to Event Hubs or storage accounts ingested through Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure with sourcetype expectations such as azure:openai or msazure:diagnostic variants your field extractions map. Normalize harm category enumerations into cat_raw before Splunk indexing using an Edge Processor or transforms if vendors rename fields between API versions.

Lane B, OpenAI Moderation API: configure the enterprise gateway or sidecar that calls /v1/moderations to log structured JSON to index=ai_ops with sourcetype=openai:api including path, flagged categories, scores, organization_id, and application labels. Strip message bodies at the logging layer; retain only length and optional salted hash when counsel approves.

Lane C, Amazon Bedrock Guardrails: ensure CloudTrail organization trails capture ApplyGuardrail management events with outcome fields, guardrail identifiers, and linked model inference context without persisting full model output in CloudTrail itself. Use Splunk Add-on for AWS for aws:cloudtrail parsing; map recipientAccountId to tenant for multisearch coalesce lanes.

Lane D, Vertex AI Gemini safety: forward Pub/Sub subscriptions or Cloud Logging sinks that include safety_attributes, blocked reasons, and finish reasons into index=ai_ops with sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety or gcp:vertex:audit depending on whether you ingest admin activity versus generative telemetry. Keep region labels because safety defaults differ by geography.

Lane E, Internal gateway moderation HEC: deploy a lightweight forwarder or OpenTelemetry collector that posts OTLP logs converted to JSON with sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation on index=ai_hec, stamping app_id, request_id, policy_engine_child, decision, and normalized violation codes. Confirm backpressure alarms exist on the collector fleet.

Lane F optional Anthropic: when Claude projects emit moderation or policy annotations through anthropic:api structured logs, include them in the thirty-day baseline subsearch categories list even if you omit a sixth multisearch arm for search budget reasons; document that compromise in runbooks.

Lookup governance: maintain llm_app_inventory.csv with columns app_id, owner, tenant, classification, is_external_facing, red_team_window refreshed from the same configuration database that provisions OAuth clients and API keys. red_team_window should be boolean-like strings consistent with Splunk eval match logic.

CIM alignment: map high-value blocked external-facing incidents into Splunk CIM Alerts where feasible so tstats correlation arms see parallel enterprise detections keyed on Alerts.dest that you harmonize with app_id through inventory normalization.

props.conf: TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, SEDCMD to strip any accidental message fields, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS aligned to vendor time. INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS only after performance testing.

Pre-save validation: run five lab prompts—benign, borderline self-harm vocabulary in medical context, simulated injection attempt in a sandbox project, multilingual news summarization with violence-related words, and a red-team window tagged development application—to confirm categories land in expected lanes without raw text.

First pipeline clause walk mirrors the saved search: the outer comment macro documents intent in plain language for auditors. multisearch opens bracketed arms in parallel; each arm ends with fields trimming to a common schema. The Azure arm binds lane azure_openai_cf, derives filter_phase, cat_raw, sev_raw, interim model field mdl, aid, rid, tnt, and act_block from finish_reason style tokens. The OpenAI moderation arm filters path or event markers, sets lane openai_moderation_api, and maps flagged booleans into act_block. The CloudTrail arm filters eventName ApplyGuardrail, sets lane bedrock_apply_guardrail, and interprets BLOCK style outcomes. The Vertex arm unions vertex:gemini-safety with gcp:vertex:audit, sets lane vertex_gemini_safety, and reads safety_category style fields. The gateway arm reads llm:gateway:moderation from ai_hec with lane internal_gateway_mod. After multisearch reunification, eval coalesce renames unify category, severity_raw, model, app_id, request_id, and tenant. join app_id pulls owner, registry_tenant renamed from inventory tenant, classification, is_external_facing, and red_team_window from llm_app_inventory.csv. streamstats time_window=3600 counts violations per rolling hour per app_id. join category pulls cat_baseline_30d from thirty-day history. eventstats averages baselines peer-wise per category. join app_id with tstats on Alerts counts cim_alert_cnt. case assigns severity_tier and recommended_action. table emits nineteen analyst columns.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation with a fifteen-minute schedule for operational review and an hourly executive digest that filters to CRITICAL and HIGH severity_tier rows. Dispatch earliest=-24h@h unless your governance window demands seven days for slower providers. Throttle duplicate app_id, category pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates from MEDIUM to CRITICAL. Route CRITICAL rows to the AI risk officer queue with links to provider admin consoles; include recommended_action text verbatim.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Azure OpenAI content filter arms, OpenAI Moderation API arms, Bedrock ApplyGuardrail CloudTrail arms, Vertex Gemini safety arms, and internal gateway moderation HEC arms so a silent lane cannot hide a policy bypass. Each arm normalizes cat_raw, sev_raw, model identifiers, app_id, request_id, and tenant keys before the outer coalesce renames unify category, severity_raw, model, app_id, request_id, and tenant. join with inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv enriches owner, registry_tenant merged back into tenant, classification, is_external_facing, and red_team_window for policy interpretation. streamstats time_window=3600 counts violations per app_id for burst detection. join on category pulls cat_baseline_30d from thirty-day history to compare current spikes against historical category volume. eventstats avg(cat_baseline_30d) builds a peer baseline column for analyst context. join with tstats on Alerts counts sibling enterprise alerts per app_id harmonized through Alerts.dest mapping. case assigns CRITICAL when an external-facing application outside an approved red-team window records a blocked event in PII, credential, or jailbreak families; HIGH for blocked self-harm or CSAM-oriented categories; MEDIUM for flagged-not-blocked elevations or internal red-team-adjacent blocks; LOW for prompt-side filtering on development applications inside red_team_window; INFO otherwise. recommended_action encodes escalation verbs for risk, legal, and product owners.

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal SLO, materialize per-lane summaries into an hourly summary index keyed on app_id and category while keeping this search for investigations. If inputlookup cardinality grows beyond a few thousand rows, migrate to KV Store with automatic pruning.

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation | where severity_tier IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.11 LLM content policy violations: correlate provider filter metadata only—categories, severities, blocked versus flagged—enrich applications, roll hourly volume, baseline categories, and join CIM Alerts without storing raw blocked completions.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=azure:openai earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="azure_openai_cf"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(trigger_phase,filter_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(content_filter_category,harm_category,policy_block_reason,annotation_category,"azure_cf_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(risk_severity,filter_severity,outcome_band,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_name,deployment_name,model_id,"unknown_model")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,calling_application,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,operation_Id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,workspace_id,subscription_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(action,result,content_filter_result,finish_reason,""),"(?i)blocked|filtered|content_filter|interrupt"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=openai:api earliest=-24h@h latest=now (path="/v1/moderations" OR moderation_endpoint="true" OR event_type="moderation")
      | eval lane="openai_moderation_api"
      | eval filter_phase="prompt_or_text_classify"
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(top_category,flagged_category,moderation_category,highest_score_category,"mod_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(score_band,moderation_severity,threshold_tier,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_used,mod_model,"text-moderation-latest")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,client_label,project_slug,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,idempotency_key,openai_request_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(organization_id,openai_org_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(flagged,blocked,"false"),"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=security sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-24h@h latest=now eventName=ApplyGuardrail
      | eval lane="bedrock_apply_guardrail"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(eval_phase,gate_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(guardrail_intervention,content_policy_action,output_filter_reason,topic_policy_hit,"bedrock_gr_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity_label,outcome_tier,guardrail_severity,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(modelId,bedrock_model_id,request_model_id,"bedrock_unknown")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_integration_tag,customer_tag,application_key,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(requestId,aws_request_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(recipientAccountId,aws_account_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(action,effect,outcome,""),"(?i)BLOCK|DENY|GUARDRAIL_INTERVENED|interrupt"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety OR sourcetype=gcp:vertex:audit) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="vertex_gemini_safety"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(safety_phase,policy_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(safety_category,harm_block_type,policy_reason,safety_attribute,"vertex_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(block_strength,risk_class,probability_band,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_version,gemini_model,publisher_model_id,"gemini_unknown")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,consumer_label,client_id,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,trace_id,span_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(project_id,billing_project,gcp_project,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(blocked,safety_blocked,finish_reason,""),"(?i)blocked|SAFETY|PROHIBITED_CONTENT|filter"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_hec sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="internal_gateway_mod"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(gateway_phase,policy_engine_child,"both")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(policy_category,ruleset_hit,siem_category,normalized_violation_code,"gw_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(rule_severity,score_class,priority_label,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(upstream_model,route_model,resolved_route,"gateway_agg")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,service_mesh_service,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,trace_token,correlation_token,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org_tenant,business_unit_code,realm_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(decision,outcome,""),"(?i)BLOCK|DENY|hard_stop"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
| eval category=coalesce(cat_raw,"unknown_cat")
| eval severity_raw=coalesce(sev_raw,"medium")
| eval model=coalesce(mdl,"unknown_model")
| eval app_id=coalesce(aid,"na")
| eval request_id=coalesce(rid,"na")
| eval tenant=coalesce(tnt,"na")
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
     | rename tenant AS registry_tenant
     | fields app_id owner registry_tenant classification is_external_facing red_team_window ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unassigned_owner")
| eval tenant=coalesce(registry_tenant,tenant)
| eval classification=coalesce(classification,"unclassified")
| eval is_external_facing=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(is_external_facing,"unknown"))))
| eval red_team_window=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(red_team_window,"false"))))
| streamstats time_window=3600 count AS viol_roll_1h BY app_id
| join type=left max=0 category
    [| search index=ai_ops earliest=-30d@d latest=now (sourcetype=azure:openai OR sourcetype=openai:api OR sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety OR sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation OR sourcetype=anthropic:api)
     | eval category=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(content_filter_category,harm_category,policy_block_reason,top_category,safety_category,policy_reason,moderation_label,"merged_cat"))))
     | stats count AS cat_baseline_30d by category ]
| eval cat_baseline_30d=coalesce(cat_baseline_30d,0)
| eventstats avg(cat_baseline_30d) AS cat_baseline_peer_avg BY category
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS cim_alert_cnt FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
     | rename Alerts.dest AS app_id
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id))) ]
| eval cim_alert_cnt=coalesce(cim_alert_cnt,0)
| eval pii_or_jailbreak=if(match(category,"(?i)pii|person|injection|jailbreak|credential"),1,0)
| eval self_harm_csam=if(match(category,"(?i)self_harm|self-harm|sexual.*minor|csam|child"),1,0)
| eval in_red_team=if(match(red_team_window,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval severity_tier=case(
    act_block==1 AND is_external_facing="true" AND in_red_team==0 AND pii_or_jailbreak==1,"CRITICAL",
    act_block==1 AND self_harm_csam==1,"HIGH",
    act_block==0 AND match(lower(severity_raw),"flag|review|warn|elevated"),"MEDIUM",
    act_block==0 AND match(filter_phase,"(?i)prompt") AND in_red_team==1,"LOW",
    act_block==1 AND in_red_team==1 AND is_external_facing="false","MEDIUM",
    true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity_tier=="CRITICAL","route_ai_risk_officer_external_pii_or_jailbreak_evidence_pack",
    severity_tier=="HIGH","invoke_trust_safety_legal_escalation_and_vendor_abuse_channel",
    severity_tier=="MEDIUM","review_guardrail_thresholds_system_prompt_and_locale_lexicon",
    true(),"archive_metadata_only_control_proof")
| table _time lane app_id owner tenant category severity_tier severity_raw model request_id filter_phase act_block viol_roll_1h cat_baseline_30d cat_baseline_peer_avg cim_alert_cnt classification is_external_facing recommended_action
```

Validation fixtures: maintain llm_policy_validation_samples.csv listing sample_request_id, expected_category, expected_lane, and expected_severity_tier after lab replay. Red-team automation should script OAuth calls through the same gateways production uses so field extractions match. Sample input might show lane azure_openai_cf with category jailbreak and act_block 1 for app_id cust_chat_prod where is_external_facing true and red_team_window false, yielding CRITICAL when pii_or_jailbreak patterns align. Sample output row must surface nineteen table columns with cim_alert_cnt possibly zero. Confirm no raw blocked text by searching for absent message, completion, choices, and text fields in indexed data after SEDCMD.

Step 4 — Validate

Positive validation: execute an approved red-team script inside a calendar-windowed project whose app_id row sets red_team_window true, confirm LOW or MEDIUM severities dominate, and capture category histograms per lane. Repeat for an external-facing production app without red_team_window using synthetic injection probes permitted by policy; expect CRITICAL when categories match pii_or_jailbreak patterns.

Provider console cross-check: in Azure AI Studio, AWS Bedrock console, Vertex AI Model Garden monitoring, and OpenAI usage dashboards, compare incident timestamps for the same request_id or trace identifiers Splunk indexes. Mismatches usually indicate field extraction drift—update FIELDALIAS before muting alerts.

Baseline sanity: confirm cat_baseline_30d is non-zero for historically chatty categories; if the thirty-day join returns null, verify the baseline subsearch indexes and sourcetype list include your Anthropic and gateway feeds.

CIM correlation: pick an app_id with nonzero cim_alert_cnt and open Enterprise Security incident review or native notable detail to confirm Alerts.dest normalization truly maps to the application inventory key; adjust CIM transforms if dest carries hostnames while inventory stores service accounts.

Negative control: pause gateway logging intentionally in a lab, confirm complementary forwarder health monitors raise ingestion gaps, then restore and verify viol_roll_1h returns.

Executive replay: export the closing table to CSV with signatures from Head of Platform for quarterly AI governance packs and regulators requesting AI Act technical documentation.

Operational dashboards: publish a Dashboard Studio or Simple XML view with lane facet, category heatmap, viol_roll_1h sparklines, and cim_alert_cnt overlays. Weekly trend reports go to product owners summarizing category drift week over week. Escalate to the AI risk officer when external-facing apps show sustained category mix shifts absent approved model upgrades.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — Azure diagnostic settings missing or Event Hub authorization expired: restore ingest using Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Azure health dashboards; replay dead-letter storage if configured; backfill summary indexes once offsets reset.

Case B — Bedrock guardrail policy disabled silently through IAM or console drift: compare CloudTrail DeleteGuardrail or UpdateGuardrail events with change tickets; re-enable policies and require MFA for guardrail mutations.

Case C — Vertex region mismatch where safety logs land in europe-west4 but Splunk search defaults to United States indexes: fix Pub/Sub subscription routing and sourcetype tagging; align retention classes per data residency.

Case D — OpenAI moderation API not called by application despite architecture diagrams showing it should: zero events from openai:api moderation path indicates code path bypass; engage developers to enforce server-side classification before streaming tokens to users.

Case E — PII redaction regex at the collector strips the literal token category along with message fields: categories appear blank and severity_tier falls to INFO; refine SEDCMD scope to fields not used for policy codes.

Case F — Provider threshold change after model upgrade shifts flagged-not-blocked histograms: correlate model version fields with release notes; update severity case thresholds and document vendor drift in the governance register.

Case G — Translation application flags benign news summarization containing violence vocabulary in a non-English locale: tune locale-specific lexicons, mark app as translation_profile in inventory, downgrade MEDIUM to INFO with approval.

Case H — red_team_window misalignment where CSV still false during approved drills: CRITICAL false alarms fire; refresh llm_app_inventory.csv from CI pipeline within minutes of schedule changes.

Case I — llm_app_inventory.csv stale so is_external_facing remains unknown: severity defaults mis-route; automate CSV regeneration from service catalog exports.

Case J — Duplicated counts when multiple providers respond to the same request_id during fan-out routing: dedupe using streamstats first or summarize by request_id before viol_roll_1h.

Case K — HEC backpressure drops high-volume moderation bursts: scale collectors, enable disk buffering, and alert on queue depth; cross-check with provider consoles for missing intervals.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five provider and gateway arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, multisearch, and tstats appear; final table lists nineteen columns for audit export.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.11 LLM content policy violations: correlate provider filter metadata only—categories, severities, blocked versus flagged—enrich applications, roll hourly volume, baseline categories, and join CIM Alerts without storing raw blocked completions.")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=azure:openai earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="azure_openai_cf"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(trigger_phase,filter_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(content_filter_category,harm_category,policy_block_reason,annotation_category,"azure_cf_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(risk_severity,filter_severity,outcome_band,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_name,deployment_name,model_id,"unknown_model")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,application_id,calling_application,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,correlation_id,operation_Id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,workspace_id,subscription_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(action,result,content_filter_result,finish_reason,""),"(?i)blocked|filtered|content_filter|interrupt"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_ops sourcetype=openai:api earliest=-24h@h latest=now (path="/v1/moderations" OR moderation_endpoint="true" OR event_type="moderation")
      | eval lane="openai_moderation_api"
      | eval filter_phase="prompt_or_text_classify"
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(top_category,flagged_category,moderation_category,highest_score_category,"mod_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(score_band,moderation_severity,threshold_tier,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_used,mod_model,"text-moderation-latest")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,client_label,project_slug,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,idempotency_key,openai_request_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(organization_id,openai_org_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(flagged,blocked,"false"),"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=security sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-24h@h latest=now eventName=ApplyGuardrail
      | eval lane="bedrock_apply_guardrail"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(eval_phase,gate_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(guardrail_intervention,content_policy_action,output_filter_reason,topic_policy_hit,"bedrock_gr_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(severity_label,outcome_tier,guardrail_severity,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(modelId,bedrock_model_id,request_model_id,"bedrock_unknown")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_integration_tag,customer_tag,application_key,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(requestId,aws_request_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(recipientAccountId,aws_account_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(action,effect,outcome,""),"(?i)BLOCK|DENY|GUARDRAIL_INTERVENED|interrupt"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_ops (sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety OR sourcetype=gcp:vertex:audit) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="vertex_gemini_safety"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(safety_phase,policy_stage,"completion")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(safety_category,harm_block_type,policy_reason,safety_attribute,"vertex_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(block_strength,risk_class,probability_band,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(model_version,gemini_model,publisher_model_id,"gemini_unknown")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,consumer_label,client_id,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,trace_id,span_id,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(project_id,billing_project,gcp_project,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(blocked,safety_blocked,finish_reason,""),"(?i)blocked|SAFETY|PROHIBITED_CONTENT|filter"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
    [ search index=ai_hec sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="internal_gateway_mod"
      | eval filter_phase=coalesce(gateway_phase,policy_engine_child,"both")
      | eval cat_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(policy_category,ruleset_hit,siem_category,normalized_violation_code,"gw_unset"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(rule_severity,score_class,priority_label,"medium"))))
      | eval mdl=coalesce(upstream_model,route_model,resolved_route,"gateway_agg")
      | eval aid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_id,service_mesh_service,"na"))))
      | eval rid=trim(toString(coalesce(request_id,trace_token,correlation_token,"na")))
      | eval tnt=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(org_tenant,business_unit_code,realm_id,"na"))))
      | eval act_block=if(match(coalesce(decision,outcome,""),"(?i)BLOCK|DENY|hard_stop"),1,0)
      | fields _time lane filter_phase cat_raw sev_raw mdl aid rid tnt act_block ]
| eval category=coalesce(cat_raw,"unknown_cat")
| eval severity_raw=coalesce(sev_raw,"medium")
| eval model=coalesce(mdl,"unknown_model")
| eval app_id=coalesce(aid,"na")
| eval request_id=coalesce(rid,"na")
| eval tenant=coalesce(tnt,"na")
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
     | rename tenant AS registry_tenant
     | fields app_id owner registry_tenant classification is_external_facing red_team_window ]
| eval owner=coalesce(owner,"unassigned_owner")
| eval tenant=coalesce(registry_tenant,tenant)
| eval classification=coalesce(classification,"unclassified")
| eval is_external_facing=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(is_external_facing,"unknown"))))
| eval red_team_window=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(red_team_window,"false"))))
| streamstats time_window=3600 count AS viol_roll_1h BY app_id
| join type=left max=0 category
    [| search index=ai_ops earliest=-30d@d latest=now (sourcetype=azure:openai OR sourcetype=openai:api OR sourcetype=vertex:gemini-safety OR sourcetype=llm:gateway:moderation OR sourcetype=anthropic:api)
     | eval category=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(content_filter_category,harm_category,policy_block_reason,top_category,safety_category,policy_reason,moderation_label,"merged_cat"))))
     | stats count AS cat_baseline_30d by category ]
| eval cat_baseline_30d=coalesce(cat_baseline_30d,0)
| eventstats avg(cat_baseline_30d) AS cat_baseline_peer_avg BY category
| join type=left max=0 app_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS cim_alert_cnt FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
     | rename Alerts.dest AS app_id
     | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id))) ]
| eval cim_alert_cnt=coalesce(cim_alert_cnt,0)
| eval pii_or_jailbreak=if(match(category,"(?i)pii|person|injection|jailbreak|credential"),1,0)
| eval self_harm_csam=if(match(category,"(?i)self_harm|self-harm|sexual.*minor|csam|child"),1,0)
| eval in_red_team=if(match(red_team_window,"(?i)true|1|yes"),1,0)
| eval severity_tier=case(
    act_block==1 AND is_external_facing="true" AND in_red_team==0 AND pii_or_jailbreak==1,"CRITICAL",
    act_block==1 AND self_harm_csam==1,"HIGH",
    act_block==0 AND match(lower(severity_raw),"flag|review|warn|elevated"),"MEDIUM",
    act_block==0 AND match(filter_phase,"(?i)prompt") AND in_red_team==1,"LOW",
    act_block==1 AND in_red_team==1 AND is_external_facing="false","MEDIUM",
    true(),"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity_tier=="CRITICAL","route_ai_risk_officer_external_pii_or_jailbreak_evidence_pack",
    severity_tier=="HIGH","invoke_trust_safety_legal_escalation_and_vendor_abuse_channel",
    severity_tier=="MEDIUM","review_guardrail_thresholds_system_prompt_and_locale_lexicon",
    true(),"archive_metadata_only_control_proof")
| table _time lane app_id owner tenant category severity_tier severity_raw model request_id filter_phase act_block viol_roll_1h cat_baseline_30d cat_baseline_peer_avg cim_alert_cnt classification is_external_facing recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev latest(Alerts.signature) AS sig count FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS app_id
| eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id)))
| join type=left max=0 app_id [| inputlookup llm_app_inventory.csv | eval app_id=lower(trim(toString(app_id))) | fields app_id classification is_external_facing owner]
| table app_id owner classification is_external_facing alert_sev sig count
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Alerts summaries for at least thirty days so tstats joins surface sibling notables correlated with LLM application identifiers; refresh llm_app_inventory.csv whenever OAuth clients or service accounts change.
visualization: Heatmap of category by lane, line chart of viol_roll_1h, overlay of cim_alert_cnt, drilldown to request_id metadata only.

references.title: Vertex AI generative AI safety attributes

knownFalsePositives: Annual red-team campaigns and purple-team replay weeks intentionally spike jailbreak and injection categories; without red_team_window tagging, severity math pages executives during sanctioned drills. ML retraining sets and evaluation harnesses emit synthetic harmful strings that light up filters even though no customer saw output; route those app_id rows to non-production indexes or enrich with dataset labels. Locale-specific false positives arise when medical charting mentions self-harm assessment language, legal contracts quote violent statutes, or trauma counseling bots use clinical vocabulary providers flag broadly. Providers may retune thresholds between API releases without a semver bump you notice immediately; pair category drift with model_id and deployment_id dimensions. Model version migrations can remap category enums so historical baselines look noisy until lookup tables translate legacy codes. Multi-provider fan-out for resilience duplicates the same logical request_id across Azure and Bedrock arms, double-counting viol_roll_1h unless deduped. Tenants indexing regulated medical or legal corpora into retrieval may see elevated PII categories from grounding snippets rather than user malice. Benign multilingual news summarization can surface violence tokens in headlines and trigger sexual or violence scores until translation-aware policies ship. Gateway middleware that truncates headers may drop category fields while leaving act_block true, confusing triage. Scheduled provider maintenance windows can zero out one multisearch arm while others continue, mimicking a bypass until health monitors fire.
hardware: Applies to cloud-hosted large language model deployments on Azure OpenAI Service, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, OpenAI enterprise APIs, Anthropic Claude workspaces accessed through approved gateways, and internal inference proxies forwarding OTLP or JSON moderation telemetry.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.10 credential rotation evidence, UC-13.4.4 model version inventory, UC-13.4.6 Ollama abuse analytics, UC-13.4.5 gateway rate limiting fairness, UC-13.4.7 MCP suspicious activity, UC-13.4.13 DSDL autoencoder lifecycle, UC-13.4.15 MLTK drift, UC-13.4.1 latency SLOs, UC-13.4.2 token cost, or UC-13.4.3 GPU telemetry. Does not automatically block prompts; it produces evidence and routing guidance for human-approved safety operations.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, send a moderated prompt that triggers a provider content filter block with category jailbreak while app_id maps to an external-facing inventory row with red_team_window false; confirm security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation returns CRITICAL with nonzero viol_roll_1h and metadata-only fields.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After disabling the synthetic test route, confirm only benign traffic remains, cim_alert_cnt returns to typical baselines, and filtering the saved search to CRITICAL yields zero rows when no genuine external policy violations occur.

evidence: Saved search security_uc_13_4_11_llm_content_policy_correlation with fifteen-minute schedule; Dashboard Studio AI safety overview; weekly CSV export to restricted governance index; alert routing records to the AI risk officer distribution list.
